creator: gpapp115@gmail.com
text:
The NFS/TFTP server is running 13.1-RELEASE (13.1-RELEASE FreeBSD 13.1-RELEASE #0 releng/13.1-n250148-fc952ac2212-dirty: Thu Jun  2 14:55:31 CEST 2022 GENERIC)
When I boot on computer number 1 which can boot FreeBSD 13.1-RELEASE from a pendrive, it succesfully fetches /boot/pxeboot but it hangs. Trying to boot computer number 2 however, it produced a crash dump.

int=0000000d  err=0000bc00  efl=00010202  eip=000000bc
eax=00000000  ebx=000000b9  ecx=0000000f  edx=00000000
esi=000000b7  edi=000000b8  ebp=000000ba  esp=00075113
cs=002b  ds=0033  es=0033    fs=0033  gs=0033  ss=0033
cs:eip=07 00 8b 40 14 85 c0 74-06 2b 05 30 c8 07 00 a3
       b4 4c 07 00 6a 38 68 3c-c8 07 00 e8 2a 3b 03 00
ss:esp=00 bc 00 00 00 bd 00 00-00 be 00 00 00 bf 00 00
       00 c0 00 00 00 c1 00 00-00 c2 00 00 00 c3 00 00
BTX halted

This report might be a duplicate of bug #257018 but I can't be sure.
